descript.abstract: Pasture is a feature class in the Rangeland Management data set. It represents the area boundaries of livestock grazing pastures. The area corresponds to tabular data in the RIMS (Rangeland Information Management System).
descript.purpose: The Pasture feature class designates boundaries to establish extent of livestock distribution and management within pastures. This is a published layer created by combining GIS data managed by each National Forest and attribute data stored in the Forest Service Infra database application. This dataset is designed for reporting and analysis and is not used to enter or edit data. The data will be used to create permit maps, field working maps and any other logical uses that the internal and external public may determine this information is helpful for.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below

dataqual.logic: Data stewards have been relied on to validate the data quality and accuracy of the boundaries for allotments, pastures, and key areas. Pasture and key area boundaries have been identified using various methods through the years like GPS or digitizing. Spatial data comes from the district and is consolidated at the forest level. This will be a consolidated national extent of the data entered at the lowest level of the agency. Data quality is dependent on quality data maintenance by local data stewards. By having the ETL create the allotment feature class from all active pastures the quality of that data will be directly dependent on the pasture data.
dataqual.complete: This data is current only as of the date of publication and may change daily. The need for a daily refresh is key to incorporate any and all local changes as they are made and keep the data in EDW fresh.

horizpa.horizpar: Positional accuracy is verified by visual comparison to source datasets

procstep.procdesc: Data is the result of a national data call where local units were asked to identify and provide spatial and tabular data to represent pastures and key areas with the intent to have the allotment feature class created based on active pastures. Local units are to follow the current GIS Data Dictionaries and load their local data into the appropriate SDE to be collected and merged into a national extent.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

The numeric code, six digits, for the Admin Organizational unit (RRFFDD where R = Region code number, F = Forest code number, and D = District code number). The administrative organization is the Ranger District on which the allotment is located.
The name associated with the Admin Organization identified.

The numeric code for the Managing Organizational unit (RRFFDD where R = Region code number, F = Forest code number, and D = District code number). The managing organization is the Ranger District that manages the allotment, regardless of the location of the allotment.
The name associated with the Managing Organization identified.
Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases. Specifically for pastures.
A 3 character alpha numeric identifier that uniquely identifies the Pasture within the Allotment.
The name of the pasture. The name need not be unique.
The subtype of the unit.

edom.edomvd: Indicates that there is no subtype beyond Pasture.

attr.attrdef: The operating status of the pasture
The number of acres within the pasture on National Forest System land, regardless of administrative unit boundaries.

attrdomv.udom: The number of acres within the pasture on National Forest System land, regardless of administrative unit boundaries.

attr.attrdef: The number of waived acres within the pasture on land that is not National Forest System land, regardless of administrative unit boundaries.

attrdomv.udom: The number of waived acres within the pasture on land that is not National Forest System land, regardless of administrative unit boundaries.

attr.attrdef: The number of acres within the pasture on land that is not National Forest System land and has not been waived, regardless of administrative unit boundaries.

attrdomv.udom: The number of acres within the pasture on land that is not National Forest System land and has not been waived, regardless of administrative unit boundaries.

attr.attrdef: The number of acres within the pasture regardless of land ownership.

attrdomv.udom: The number of acres within the pasture regardless of land ownership.

attr.attrdef: The area of the feature in acres, calculated in North American Albers Equal Area Conic projection (NAD83) meters and then converted to acres with the formula AreaMeters * 0.0002471054.
Indicator to identify if the pasture was administered to standard.

edom.edomvd: Pasture was not administered to standard

attr.attrdef: The fiscal year the pasture was last reported as administered to standard.

attrdomv.udom: The fiscal year the pasture was last reported as administered to standard. (YYYY)

attr.attrdef: Indicator to identify if the standards were met at the end of the season.

edom.edomvd: It is uncertain if standards were exceeded or not.

attr.attrdef: The fiscal year the pasture was last reported for end of season compliance. (YYYY)

attrdomv.udom: The fiscal year the pasture was last reported for end of season compliance. (YYYY)

attr.attrdef: Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases. Specifically for allotments.
A 5 character alpha numeric identifier assigned to the allotment.
The name of the allotment. The name need not be unique.

Revision date, the date and time when the feature was created or last updated. Time is entered using a 24-hour clock (military time) based on the Pacific Standard Time (PST) zone. Tasks associated with each revision should be documented in the process steps of theme-based metadata. Entering the date only is acceptable.
Feature data source code that describes how the data feature was derived or collected.
Distance (+/-), in dataset distance units, of the expected difference between feature and real-world object locations.
Globally Unique Identifier assigned to the spatial record. Primary key for linking geospatial objects with other database systems.
Feature geometry.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
